Subject: Check-in Call

What.Name: Scottish & Southern Electricity Networks - Khoros - Renewal - 1707763 - 7_2026

Description:
Call was in customer Teams account, no recording attendant allowed. Manual transcription.

Meeting Summary

Meeting date and time: April 2, 2026
Meeting attendees: Tim Courtenay (VP of Sales, Trilogy/IgniteTech), Kirsty Mackle (Junior Contracts Manager, SSEN).

Talking points:

Introduction and Roles: Tim explained the relationship between Trilogy, IgniteTech, and Khoros. Kirsty confirmed her role as a Junior Contracts Manager in procurement, focusing on the renewal itself, while Bradley is the primary user and business unit holder.

Status of Renewal: Kirsty stated that the Khoros renewal is currently progressing through internal discussions and is with the SSEN legal team. She noted, "it is going ahead, it is progressing."

Compliance and Documentation: Tim referenced documentation sent by Asad on March 23rd, including the DTIA and business continuity excerpt. Kirsty will verify with legal (Sophie Graham and Mark Williams) if anything further is required.

Contract Notice Period: Tim reminded Kirsty of the 90-day notice period in the current contract.

Pricing and Quotes: Tim noted that a formal renewal quote hasn't been sent yet. Kirsty will check if Bradley received an early offer, but Tim will provide a fresh quote following the call.

Entitlement Changes: Tim highlighted changes in the Khoros packaging. The renewal includes a move to "unlimited seats" and a shift from the old "Premium" support plan to the new "Success" plans (Standard, Gold, Platinum).

Support Requirements: A key decision point is whether SSEN requires 24/7 support (Platinum Success). Kirsty noted that Bradley would need to confirm this requirement.

AI Features: Tim introduced the new generative AI add-on features. Kirsty mentioned that their current "go-to" stance is to keep things similar to the current setup and not switch on AI unless needed, stating, "I think we're just wanting to keep it as similar to what we have currently."

Administrative Updates: The contracting entity address for Khoros LLC has changed to the corporate headquarters in Austin, Texas. Kirsty will check if this needs updating in their systems.

Timeline: Tim proposed finalizing commercials in early May and closing all paperwork by late June for the July 30th renewal date.

Renewal Term: Kirsty indicated they are likely looking at a one-year renewal. Tim informed her that a one-year renewal would see a price increase, whereas a three-year renewal could flatten the price and remove usage-based limits.

Insights:

Likelihood of Renewing: High. Kirsty explicitly stated that the renewal is "going ahead" and is currently with legal for terms and conditions review.

Renewal Drivers: The renewal seems driven by the critical nature of the use case (customer care during emergencies/storms). The final package (support level and AI) will be determined by Bradley.

Policy Constraints: The customer's preference for a 1-year renewal conflicts with our desire for longer-term stability; Tim has already "sensitized" her to the fact that a 1-year term will result in a price increase.

Use Case Insight: The platform is a critical customer interface tool used during emergency power situations and storm aftermaths.

Actions from the Meeting:

Provide a renewal quote (one-year and three-year options) to Kirsty and Bradley - Tim Courtenay - ASAP.

Send follow-up notes/record of the meeting - Tim Courtenay - by April 4th/5th.

Check with legal (Sophie and Mark) regarding outstanding compliance docs - Kirsty Mackle - April 8th.

Verify if an early renewal quote was already received - Kirsty Mackle - April 8th.

Check and update the Khoros LLC contracting address in the SSEN system - Kirsty Mackle - April 8th.

Review the new packaging/support levels and AI interest with Bradley - Tim/Kirsty/Bradley - Week of April 13th.

Schedule follow-up call - Tim Courtenay - Target week of April 13th.

Summary for the Salesforce Opportunity Description field
Renewal call between Tim Courtenay (Trilogy) and Kirsty Mackle (SSEN procurement) to discuss the Khoros renewal due July 30th. SSEN confirmed the renewal is progressing through legal. Discussion covered updated packaging (unlimited seats), the shift to "Success" support plans, and the introduction of AI features. Tim highlighted that a 1-year renewal will involve a price increase, while a 3-year term offers price protection and removed usage limits.

Actions:

Provide renewal quotes (1yr vs 3yr) - Tim Courtenay.

Send meeting notes - Tim Courtenay.

Check compliance docs status with legal - Kirsty Mackle.

Confirm received quotes and update vendor address - Kirsty Mackle.

Review support levels and AI with Bradley - Tim/Kirsty/Bradley.

Schedule next call for week of April 13th - Tim Courtenay.

Sales next step summary
Send 1-year and 3-year renewal quotes and schedule a follow-up call with Bradley and Kirsty to finalize the support level and commercial terms. 04/02/2024 Tim Courtenay.

Probable Outcome

Probable Outcome: Likely to Win

Probable Outcome Notes: Net risk is low. The customer confirmed the renewal is already in the legal review phase and described Khoros as a critical tool for emergency customer support. The main variables are the final support tier and the term length.

Follow-up email:

Subject: Follow-up: SSEN / Khoros Renewal Discussion

Hi Kirsty,

It was a pleasure meeting you today to discuss the upcoming Khoros renewal for SSEN.

Following our conversation, I have summarized the key points we discussed:

Renewal Status: You confirmed that the renewal process is underway internally and is currently being reviewed by your legal team.

Compliance & Documentation: We noted the DTIA and business continuity documentation sent by Asad on March 23rd. You will be checking with Sophie Graham and Mark Williams to see if any further information is required from our side.

Contracting Entity: We discussed the address change for Khoros LLC. Please let me know if you require any specific documentation to update this in your vendor system.

Updated Packaging: I highlighted that the new Khoros packaging now includes unlimited seats. We also touched on the transition from "Premium Support" to our new "Success" tiers (Standard, Gold, and Platinum).

AI Features: We introduced the new generative AI capabilities. While SSEN’s current stance is to keep the setup consistent with the current implementation, these features remain available as an opt-in add-on.

Renewal Terms: We discussed the July 30th renewal date and the 90-day notice period. I noted that while a one-year renewal will see a price increase, a three-year commitment would allow us to flatten that pricing and remove usage-based limits (seats, visits, API calls).

Next Steps & Actions:

Tim Courtenay: I will send over a formal renewal quote providing both one-year and three-year options for your review.

Kirsty Mackle: You will check with your legal team regarding compliance docs and verify the current vendor address on your system.

Tim/Kirsty/Bradley: We will aim to meet during the week of April 13th to review the specific requirements for support levels and discuss Bradley's feedback on the new entitlements.

Please let me know if there is anything else you would like to add to these notes. I look forward to our next discussion.

Best regards,

Tim Courtenay
VP of Sales, Trilogy

Subject: Check-in Call
Description:
Meeting Summary
* Meeting date and time: 04/01/2026
* Meeting attendees:
* Sebastian Desand (Trilogy Renewals / Khoros)
* Eric Sharrow (H&R Block – Procurement)
* Meeting length: ~36 minutes
Talking points:
1. Purpose of call:
* Early-stage renewal discussion for Khoros Care ahead of July 31st renewal date.
* Focus on understanding current usage, renewal intent, and introducing new offerings.
2. Customer current position on renewal:
* Likely to renew but not fully committed.
* Customer stated: “I would probably… like 80%… maybe even like 90.”
* Decision will depend heavily on pricing and internal alignment.
* Customer emphasized transparency: will not push for concessions unless confident deal will close.
3. Current contract and usage:
* Approx. $100K annual contract value
* 20 seats currently in use
* No immediate need for additional licenses
* Usage largely unchanged from previous year
4. Customer feedback on product:
* Positive on usability:
* “They truly enjoy the interface… it makes sense.”
* Historical concern:
* Poor support responsiveness and account turnover (during prior RFP phase)
* No recent complaints, but status needs validation post-tax season.
5. Internal customer situation:
* Organizational changes:
* Team restructuring (downsizing and role changes)
* New reporting line under VP of IT (Paul Clark)
* Key stakeholder: Patrick Pearl (Director)
* Decision-making still stabilizing.
6. Timing constraints:
* Business heavily impacted by tax season (until April 15th)
* Meaningful engagement expected late April / early May
* Renewal deadline: July 31st
7. Pricing sensitivity:
* Customer explicitly concerned about potential increase:
* If pricing increases significantly, may revisit RFP / alternative vendors
* Sebastian confirmed pricing will remain “in the ballpark” (not a major increase).
8. New commercial model introduced:
* Shift to bundle/enterprise model:
* Unlimited seats
* Additional products included (Khoros One, Community)
* Pricing not dependent on usage of entitlements.
9. Khoros One discussion:
* Customer not familiar with offering
* Strong interest in learning more, especially:
* Marketing use cases
* Cross-team value (beyond current Care use case)
* Customer sees opportunity to expand usage across business units.
10. Demo and enablement:
* Customer requested materials (links, docs) to share internally
* Goal: build interest before scheduling demo
* Potential to include marketing team stakeholders in evaluation.
1. Contracting discussion:
* Supplier wants to move customer to standardized contract platform
* Customer prefers to stay on existing terms if possible (to avoid delays)
* Legal review required on both sides.

Insights:
* Renewal likelihood is high but not guaranteed:
* Strong baseline dependency on product
* Risk tied primarily to pricing increases
* Customer is procurement-led and cost-conscious:
* Will re-open competitive process if pricing is unfavorable
* Expansion opportunity:
* Khoros One + enterprise model could unlock cross-functional adoption (marketing team)
* Timing risk:
* Limited engagement before mid-April due to tax season
* Contracting risk:
* Moving to new legal terms may slow or complicate renewal
* Key takeaway:
* This is a defensive renewal with upsell potential, but success depends on:
* Controlled pricing
* Effective internal enablement (materials + demo)

Actions from the Meeting
* Sebastian Desand:
* Send Khoros One materials / links to Eric – ASAP
* Prepare and send renewal presentation – ASAP
* Provide contract/T&Cs (pending legal review) – TBD
* Review existing contract + amendment with legal – ASAP
* Eric Sharrow:
* Share materials with Patrick and internal stakeholders – ASAP
* Identify marketing stakeholders for potential demo – TBD
* Provide post-tax season feedback on usage and support experience – late April
* Both:
* Schedule demo (likely late April / early May)
* Continue renewal discussions with pricing – TBD

Subject: Cancellation Call

What.Name: Virginia Rural Water Association (VRWA) - GoMembers - Renewal - 7_2026

Description:
Meeting Summary
Meeting date and time: Not specified
Meeting attendees: James Stothard (Supplier), Lisa Kerr (Customer – Virginia Rural Water)
Meeting length: ~11 minutes

Talking points:

Renewal discussion for GoMembers ahead of contract end date (1st July) and termination deadline (1st May).
Customer had previously submitted a cancellation request but withdrew it due to misunderstanding that cancellation would be immediate. Clarified that cancellation stops renewal/sales activity but access continues until contract end (1st July).
Customer confirmed they have not reviewed new pricing and only received a standard renewal notice without pricing details.
Customer confirmed decision to move away from GoMembers is effectively final: “Yes.”
Reason for churn is not product functionality, but:
“continual price increases and lack of support”
“anything that has to be done now… Rebecca just has to figure it out on her own”
No meaningful support or account management “for a couple of years”
Sales rep explained new pricing structure:
New minimum price: $25,600/year
Customer currently paying ~$5,000/year
Increase driven by unsustainable financial model and need to fund support and development
Customer acknowledged pricing rationale but confirmed decision to leave remains unchanged.
Customer has been preparing migration for 1.5–2 years, led by Rebecca:
Working with external provider
Gradual transition to avoid risk
Testing components and redesigning website already underway
Migration is in progress but not fully complete; some risk of “hiccups” acknowledged.
Sales rep highlighted no short-term extensions available (minimum 1-year renewal), advising customer to complete migration before July.
Customer confirmed internal awareness and board alignment on moving platforms and associated investment.
Agreement that customer will resend cancellation notice; sales rep will facilitate and confirm continued access until July 1st.

Insights:

Customer is highly unlikely to renew; decision has been in progress for up to 2 years and is already operationalized.
Primary churn drivers:
Lack of support and customer success engagement over multiple years
Price increases without perceived value increase
Customer explicitly stated: “the support… definitely has been lacking” and linked this directly to their decision.
Migration is already underway and near completion, reducing likelihood of reversal.
New pricing ($25.6k vs $5k) would represent a >5x increase, further reinforcing churn.
No indication that improved support or commercial concessions would recover the account at this stage.
Customer expectations around support would likely not be fully recoverable in short term even if addressed now.
No unrealistic requests made (e.g., discounts, short-term renewal), but customer would not accept standard pricing or terms regardless.
Actions from the Meeting
Customer (Lisa Kerr / Rebecca):
Resend cancellation notice – ASAP (before May 1st deadline)
Continue migration and testing of new platform – before July 1st
Supplier (James Stothard):
Offer to forward cancellation internally and ensure processing – upon receipt
Send email confirmation that access will remain until July 1st after cancellation submission

Subject: Check-in Call

What.Name: Egon Zehnder International AG - Messageone - Renewal - 8_2026

Description:
Call Summary – Egon, Sender International AG (EMS / MessageOne)

Attendees:

Egon (Sender International AG)
Desh Deepak
James Stothard
Situation Overview

Customer is high-risk (active churn consideration) driven by a prolonged breakdown in support and account engagement.

Key Issues
1. Support Failure
Multiple open technical cases with no resolution
No responses from support or prior contacts (Joe, Kathy, Tony, Omar)
Escalations—including to CEO level—received no response
Customer unable to progress or escalate issues
2. Account Management Gap
Customer unaware of current AM (Jamie Alvarez)
No proactive outreach from Aurea/Trilogy side
Perception of abandonment
3. Product Confidence Risk
Customer believed MessageOne may no longer be supported
Triggered market evaluation of alternatives
Renewal Context
Renewal Date: August 20
Termination Deadline: June 21
Budget Status: Already allocated for this cycle
Customer Position:
Willing to renew if confidence is restored
Otherwise prepared to switch vendors
Open Questions / Risks
Support model clarity and ownership going forward
Status and resolution plan for all open tickets
Product roadmap visibility (to confirm long-term viability)
Data storage usage clarity
Pricing consistency vs prior invoices
Decisions Made
Introduce Desh → Jamie Alvarez (Account Manager)
Schedule product roadmap + renewal alignment meeting
Customer to share meeting notes
Customer to complete sustainability questionnaire (vendor categorization)
Agreed Next Steps
Immediate (Critical)
Introduce Jamie Alvarez to customer
Schedule joint call (AM + Product team) before June 21 deadline
Preparation Required (Internal)

Validate:
Usage + data storage levels
Renewal pricing vs historical invoices
Risk Assessment
Severity: High
Drivers: Trust erosion, support failure, no ownership
Recovery Path:
Strong, coordinated response from AM + Product
Immediate visibility + action on support backlog
Clear commitment to product continuity
Key Insight

This is not a pricing or product issue—this is a trust and ownership failure.
Recovery depends on demonstrating responsiveness, accountability, and continuity within the next interaction.

Subject: Enterprise Renewal Call
Description:
Meeting Summary
* Meeting date: 03/30/2026
* Meeting attendees:
* Sebastian Desand (Trilogy Renewals)
* Robert Werner (Procurement – Global IT Software & Services)
* Anton Hallinger (IT/Technical)
* Anusha Govindu (IT-Lead for community platform)
* Meeting length: ~29 minutes
Talking points:
1. Introduction of stakeholder structure on customer side:
* Procurement, IT, and Contract/Vendor Management operate jointly in renewal decisions.
2. Supplier presented renewal options for Khoros Community:
* Current contract: 36 months including:
* 20M monthly API calls
* 8.75M annual visits
* 190 success services
* Proposed 25% price increase across all customers, mandated by leadership due to unsustainable pricing model.
3. Justification for price increase:
* Khoros financial model deemed unsustainable.
* Investment required for future development (primarily Aurora platform).
* Increase applied globally, not customer-specific.
4. Renewal options presented:
* 1-year option:
* Includes free Aurora upgrade (valued ~100K)
* Increased API and visit limits
* 3-year option (Ultimate tier):
* Unlimited API calls, visits, and AI usage
* Price lock for 3 years
* Includes Aurora upgrade and AI capabilities
5. Discussion on Khoros One (care and marketing platform):
* Customer previously used it but stated:
* “It did not pay anything for us back.”
* Open to re-evaluating if improvements demonstrated.
* Interest in demo, especially regarding AI capabilities and integration with Aurora.
6. Clarification on Aurora platform:
* Successor to Khoros Classic.
* Khoros Classic confirmed as:
* “feature complete”
* No further development, only maintenance and bug fixes.
7. Customer position on strategy:
* Will remain on Khoros Classic for at least 1 more year.
* Migration decision (Aurora or alternative vendor) expected after internal planning cycle (post-May, new fiscal year from September).
* Migration not feasible in short term due to budget and planning constraints.
8. Customer pushback on pricing:
* Strong objection to price increase for a declining product:
* “There needs to be a price decline… [as] there’s no investment anymore happening.”
* Requested pricing to reflect reduced product value.
* Position: only willing to consider 1-year renewal at reduced cost.
9. Supplier response to pricing objection:
* Confirmed no flexibility on 25% increase, as it is a global mandate.
* No ability to discount or adjust pricing, even for unchanged scope.
10. Contractual discussion:
* Customer questioned whether prior contract limited renewal increases.
* Supplier clarified limits applied only within current term, not to new contract.
Timeline:
* Renewal date: August 5th
* Customer expects to have clearer strategic direction by May.

Insights:
* Customer is not currently considering churn in the immediate term, but is actively evaluating future platform options.
* Short-term intent: 1-year renewal only, purely to maintain operations.
* Strong resistance to pricing model due to:
* Product being in maintenance mode (“declining product”)
* Lack of ongoing investment in Khoros Classic
* Customer explicitly expects price reduction, which we cannot accommodate.
* This creates significant commercial tension and renewal risk beyond 1 year.
* Customer is open to Aurora and Khoros One, but only after internal strategic alignment.
* The Aurora migration incentive (free upgrade) may lose impact if not aligned with customer timing.
* Renewal likelihood:
* Short-term (1-year): moderate to high (operational dependency)
* Long-term: at risk due to active market evaluation

Actions from the Meeting
* Sebastian Desand: Send renewal presentation to customer – ASAP
* Sebastian Desand: Arrange follow-up call after customer internal review – TBD
* Customer (Procurement/IT team): Review renewal options and pricing internally – before next discussion
* Customer: Define strategic direction (Aurora vs alternatives) – expected by May

Subject: Renewal Call
Description:
03-30-2026 Fredrik Scheike: The meeting opened to review DodFinder's upcoming renewal, price-lock and term options, and the Prime program. Participants reported wanted product features that affect daily use, message forwarding, richer text formatting, and personalized signatures with logos, which they said they had requested previously. Fredrik acknowledged he didn't know if these features would be available but would ask product management, and clarified that he handles renewals while account and product teams will manage post-contract work. Attendees described current usage as daily ticketing for customer communications and noted they use fewer agent seats than licensed, prompting a desire to reduce licenses and review renewal pricing.

The discussion then addressed pricing, licensing constraints, and AI. Fredrik explained that annual price increases are system-driven for financial stability and that the company enforces a no-reduction policy on license counts, leaving renewal or cancellation as options. He offered multi-year price-locks to avoid automated increases, which the customer needed to think about because the quoted renewal already reflects an increase versus last year’s bill. Fredrik offered to open a support ticket with central finance to resolve the customer's inability to add a credit card for payments. The group also reviewed Kayako's AI: the customer had auto-enabled AI, turned it off due to reopening tickets and inefficiency, and Fredrik committed to bring forth the feedback and bring Kayako's AI roadmap for evaluation. The commercial options and timeline were reviewed, including Prime credits, and the customer requested time to decide with a follow-up on April 24 and a May 14 cancellation deadline before the July 14 renewal. They have requested a 5 year quote to be sent to them.

Subject: Cancellation Call
Description:
Meeting Summary
* Meeting date and time: 03/27/2026
* Meeting attendees:
    * Sebastian Desand (Trilogy Renewals)
    * Ai Leen Yeoh (RHB – Head of Analytics and CRM Track)
    * Meen Dalton (System Owner, ONYX)
* Meeting length: ~8 minutes
Talking points:
1. Customer confirmed decision not to renew ONYX is final.
    * Customer stated: “It is final.”
2. Customer is moving away from ONYX to an internally developed solution.
    * They are building their own tool to replace ONYX.
3. Key reasons for non-renewal:
    * Lack of future product roadmap: customer stated they were informed that ONYX would not have further development.
    * Strategic move to cloud environment.
    * Previous RFP process conducted, where Aurea participated, but customer chose to move forward independently.
4. Renewal terms clarified by supplier:
    * Renewal date: July 1st
    * Cancellation deadline: May 1st (60-day notice period)
    * Minimum contract term: 1 year (no shorter-term options available)
5. Customer had already indicated intent to not renew to account manager (Jamie), but formal cancellation may not yet be submitted.
    * Customer to confirm internally with procurement.
6. Customer requested guidance on formal cancellation process.
    * Supplier confirmed cancellation can be done via email (no formal letter required).
    * Sebastian to send cancellation email instructions.
7. Customer raised concern about post-termination data access:
    * Asked whether they can still access system (even in limited mode) to retrieve historical data.
    * Specifically mentioned possible search-only access.
    * Acknowledged they understand they cannot use full functionality (e.g., lead management).
8. Supplier response on data access:
    * Sebastian to check internally with Jamie and revert with details on data extraction and access.
Insights:
* Customer’s likelihood to renew is extremely low.
    * Explicit statement: “It is final.”
* Decision is driven by strategic and product limitations, not pricing.
* Key drivers:
    * Lack of product roadmap
    * Need for cloud-based solution
* Customer has already progressed significantly in transition planning, indicating low likelihood of reversal.
* Customer showed no interest in extending for transition support, even when prompted.
* Customer inquired about short-term renewal options, which we cannot support (minimum 1 year term).
* Data access post-termination is a critical concern and may impact customer experience during offboarding.

Actions from the Meeting
* Sebastian Desand: Send cancellation email instructions to customer – ASAP
* Sebastian Desand: Check with Jamie regarding data access and extraction options post-termination – ASAP
* Ai Leen Yeoh / Procurement Team: Confirm and submit formal cancellation notice before May 1st

Subject: Renewal Call
Description:
Meeting Summary
Meeting date and time: Not specified in transcript
Meeting attendees:
Supplier: James Stothard (Trilogy/Aurea – Renewals)
Customer (CPKC): Suleman Ahmad (Software Procurement), Arun Jeyasingh (Content & Automation), Abdul (Senior Manager, IS), Abhi Walia (Team Lead, ECM Applications)
Meeting length: ~36 minutes

Talking points:

Renewal overview and pricing
Current renewal option via portal: ~$60,000 for 3 years (~$20,100/year).
Auto-renewal clause: contract will auto-renew if not cancelled by May 1st.
Customer flagged ~25% price increase, which is a major concern.
Suleman: “that 25% is a significant increase… we don't have the budget.”
Supplier indicated pricing flexibility is limited as it is finance-driven, but will review.
Customer procurement strategy (cost reduction initiative)
Organization-wide initiative to optimize costs across all vendors following acquisition.
Suleman: “we're looking at our cost options… trying to reduce where we can and maximize what we have.”
Renewal discussion will be cost-driven and competitive.
Product usage and current state
Product: IRIS (on-prem email marketing tool).
Use cases:
Public website subscriptions
Customer station application
Other internal applications
Usage is functional and stable: “it's all working okay.”
Customer believes they are not fully utilizing the platform.
Support issues (major concern)
Customer experienced lack of access to support portal despite active subscription (valid until July 2026).
Required escalation via LinkedIn and direct outreach.
Non-production environment has been down for months, still unresolved.
Suleman: “it did impact our operations, which is not acceptable.”
This issue has been escalated internally to leadership.
Product limitations and roadmap concerns
IRIS is on-prem only, no cloud/SaaS version available.
No clear roadmap for cloud migration.
Supplier confirmed:
No SaaS version planned
Product will continue as long as customer base exists
Customer questioned long-term viability and roadmap visibility.
Licensing model
Simple model: unlimited emails, contacts, and full platform access.
Only variable: Standard vs Platinum success (services layer).
Platinum includes:
Funnel optimization
Server configuration tuning
Service and enablement gaps
Customer requested:
Product demo / walkthrough of capabilities
Better understanding of features and upgrades
Usage reporting (unclear if available)
Contractual clarifications
May 1: deadline to cancel, not to modify terms.
July 1: renewal date, changes can still be made until then.
Auto-renewal does not bypass need for PO on customer side.
Renewal requires new license keys.
Insights
Renewal likelihood: At risk due to pricing and support issues.
Customer is under strict cost optimization pressure, evaluating all vendors.
Support failure is a major risk factor, escalated internally and impacting trust.
Lack of cloud offering and roadmap clarity weakens competitive position.
Customer is open to continuing but needs:
Pricing relief
Improved support reliability
Better product enablement
High-risk requests / likely non-compliant with policy:
Price reduction / discount (customer cannot accept 25% increase)
Potential shorter-term contract (implied if budget constraints persist)
Actions from the Meeting
James (Supplier)
Investigate and resolve non-production environment issue (urgent)
Review support failure root cause and provide explanation
Share account manager (Jay) contact details
Arrange product demo / deep dive session with product team (target: next week)
Confirm availability of usage reporting
Review pricing options / possible concessions
Send renewal quote including SLA details
Suleman (Customer)
Share email thread (Feb 6) regarding support issue
Confirm and circulate attendee emails for follow-up
Customer team (Arun / Abdul / Abhi)
Validate current system issues (non-prod status)
Review product capabilities in upcoming demo

Subject: Check-in Call

What.Name: NTT Data Americas, Inc - Messageone - Renewal - 9_2026

Description:
read.ai | NTT Data Americas, Inc. - AlertFind (MessageOne) Renewal (Meet Meeting) | Thu. Mar 26, 2026

Full Notes and Transcript: https://app.read.ai/analytics/meetings/01KMNC5YW8VQNZG4PAV5T5H5QM

Meeting ID: 01KMNC5YW8VQNZG4PAV5T5H5QM

4 Participants | James Quigley, Kiran Arora, Ron Howell, SHOBHIT

----

Summary:

The meeting opened as an introductory renewal discussion for AlertFind (MessageOne) ahead of the September 16 renewal date and focused on operational stability, functionality, and commercial questions from NTT. NTT described recent incidents where SMS messages failed or were delayed and noted slow or delayed support responses routed through an AI assistant; the group asked whether there have been product enhancements, version changes, or planned Gen AI integrations to address these issues. Participants also sought clearer pricing, discount structures for an existing customer, and demonstrations of complementary products or workflows that could reduce manual effort.

James Quigley, the vendor representative, acknowledged the concerns and said he would escalate them to product and support teams and follow up with detailed answers and pricing ahead of a scheduled follow-up meeting. He explained the current legal and support structure—MessageOne LLC operating under Trilogy/Jigsaw/ESW Operations—and outlined a "prime program" option that can bundle two additional products, Contently and Kayako, potentially at no additional cost. Outstanding items include the vendor's detailed technical responses, definitive pricing, and any demos for complementary products.

----

Chapters & Topics:

Introductions and meeting setup
Renewal context and high-level questions
-NTT raised multiple stability incidents with AlertFind, including SMS delivery failures and slow support responses, and requested investigation.
-James committed to escalating technical and support concerns to the product team and returning with comprehensive answers.
Stability and functionality concerns
-NTT asked whether AlertFind has seen recent enhancements or planned Gen AI integration.
Vendor responses and organizational structure
-James described the vendor structure and confirmed MessageOne LLC/Trilogy as the legal and support entities for the product.
Commercial options and PRIME program
-James introduced a "prime program" that could include Contently and Kayako as complementary products potentially available at no additional cost.
-NTT requested commercial clarity on renewal pricing, including one-, three-, and five-year options and possible discounts for an existing customer.
Scheduling the follow-up meeting and collateral requests
-The parties agreed to a follow-up meeting on Tuesday, March 31 at 8:00 AM Eastern to review pricing and supporting information.
Closing and next steps

----

Action Items:

-James Quigley will escalate NTT's stability and support concerns to the product and support teams and report back with comprehensive answers.
-James Quigley will introduce NTT to the portfolio director of the product by return email to provide roadmap and AI integration details.
-James Quigley will provide pricing for one-, three-, and five-year renewal options and information about discounts and competitiveness by the Tuesday follow-up.
-James Quigley will gather collateral and information on the PRIME program and the Contently and Kayako products and share it with NTT ahead of or at the Tuesday meeting.
-James Quigley will send a calendar invite for the Tuesday meeting (March 31) at 8:00 AM Eastern to all participants.

----

Key Questions:

-Are there restrictions preventing SMS to Australian numbers and why were messages not sent?
-Have there been any enhancements to AlertFind or is NTT running on an old version?
-Who is the current legal owner or parent company of MessageOne/AlertFind?
-What is the general process for renegotiation and will you provide pricing options?
-What complementary products or perks are included in the PRIME program?

Subject: Cancellation Call
Description:
read.ai | Pivotal Renewal Catch-up - S&A Homes (Meet Meeting) | Thu. Mar 26, 2026

Full Notes and Transcript: https://app.read.ai/analytics/meetings/01KMN734J8G2KRDQTWJJCYX8GA

Meeting ID: 01KMN734J8G2KRDQTWJJCYX8GA

2 Participants | James Quigley, Michael Long

----

Summary:

The meeting reviewed the Pivotal renewal status in light of the 60-day termination deadline on April 1 and confirmed next steps for cancellation and migration. James clarified that cancellation must be submitted in writing and asked for an email or an email copied to the cancellations address so Trilogy can process it, and Michael confirmed he had already sent the cancellation notice. The group discussed the reason for non-renewal: Pivotal discontinued the "home builder" vertical years ago, which forced S&A Homes to adopt a third-party solution and created persistent fit and support problems that motivated the decision not to renew.

James explained the cancellations team will close the account and proactively contact S&A Homes to confirm the implications of cancellation, and he noted the customer will retain platform access through the end of May to allow migration. James also offered to reopen renewal discussions if S&A Homes’ migration plans change, and Michael agreed to reach out if that becomes necessary.

----

Chapters & Topics:

Meeting start and logistics
Renewal purpose and deadline
-The meeting focused on the upcoming 60-day termination deadline of April 1 for the Pivotal renewal.
Cancellation decision and reasons
-S&A Homes decided not to renew Pivotal and intends to request cancellation.
-Cancellations must be submitted in writing by email and copied to the cancellations inbox for legal/internal processing.
-Michael reported he already sent the cancellation email.
-S&A Homes stopped receiving the home builder vertical, which forced use of a third party and led executives to conclude the product no longer fit their needs.
Cancellation logistics and platform access
-The customer will have access to the Pivotal platform through the end of May while they migrate away.
Contingency and closeout
-James offered to reopen renewal if migration does not proceed as planned, and Michael agreed to contact him if they need to revisit.

----

Action Items:

-Michael Long will contact James Quigley if S&A Homes needs to revisit the renewal or requires assistance during migration.
-James Quigley will forward the customer's cancellation email to the cancellations team today or tomorrow.

----

Key Questions:

-Can you send the cancellation in writing to satisfy the legal/internal requirement?
-Can you confirm the reason S&A Homes chose not to renew Pivotal?

Subject: Renewal Call
Description:
Meeting Summary

Meeting date and time: Not specified
Meeting attendees: James Stothard (Trilogy), Andy Jentges, Michael (surname not provided)
Meeting length: ~10 minutes

Talking points:

Purpose of call was a renewal check-in for TradeBeam ahead of contract expiry on 2nd July.
James explained Trilogy’s role in handling renewals and contract management for TradeBeam (via IgniteTech group).
Customer confirmed no issues with the product: “I don't think we have anything to mention.”
Customer highlighted that historically renewals have been automatic via invoice and queried if the process had changed.
James clarified that annual review is standard, including contract confirmation and pricing updates.
Customer confirmed they use TradeBeam as a supplier due to a requirement from their customer.
Customer identified Boston Scientific as the driving customer.
James confirmed Boston Scientific is contracted on TradeBeam until December 28, 2028.
Customer stated: “Yes, I would anticipate continue supplying them,” indicating ongoing dependency on TradeBeam.
Pricing shared: renewal price just over $6,200 with annual increase policy.
James proposed a 3-year term to lock in current pricing, estimating savings of ~$3,000 over the period.
Customer responded positively but wants to confirm with Boston Scientific before committing: “I'd probably just want to make a call to the customer and just substantiate.”
Agreement for James to send both 1-year and 3-year quote options.
Key dates confirmed:
Renewal date: July 2
Cancellation deadline: May 2 (60-day notice)
No product issues, risks, or complaints raised.

Insights:

Customer sentiment is neutral to positive; no dissatisfaction or risk signals regarding the product.
Renewal is dependency-driven (customer requirement), which strongly supports retention.
Likelihood of renewal is high, contingent on Boston Scientific continuing the relationship (which is confirmed through 2028).
Customer is open to multi-year commitment, indicating stability and willingness to optimize pricing.
No resistance to pricing increase was expressed.
No requests made that conflict with standard renewal policies.

Actions from the Meeting

James Stothard: Send renewal quotes for both 1-year and 3-year terms – ASAP
Andy Jentges: Confirm with Boston Scientific their continued use of TradeBeam – before decision, ideally prior to May 2 cancellation deadline

Subject: Cancellation Call
Description:
Meeting attendees: James Stothard (Supplier), Kathryn Alger (Customer)
Meeting length: ~16 minutes

Talking points:

Customer confirmed that the contract with Sprout Social has been signed during Kathryn’s absence, finalising the decision to move away from PostBeyond.
Kathryn stated clearly that “come the end of June, we won’t be using PostBeyond anymore.”
Cancellation process discussed – Kathryn will submit a formal termination email and expects to complete this ahead of the deadline (end of April).
Supplier confirmed cancellation process includes a confirmation bot and that access will remain until 30th June, with an additional data retention period (approx. 30 days).
Customer has already begun preparations for transition, including:
Requesting and receiving historical newsletter analytics/data exports
Planning internal data extraction before platform access ends
Migration to Sprout Social:
Onboarding process and support materials already provided by Sprout
Customer expects a crossover period between platforms before June
Internal rollout will include training, phased user migration, and internal communications
Customer highlighted scale of rollout: ~220 users currently on PostBeyond.
Key operational challenge identified:
Managing user migration timing and communications to avoid confusion
Training users prior to granting access to new platform
Product comparison: Customer noted platforms are broadly similar in capability; main difference is user permission structure (no “content creator” role in Sprout).
Primary reason for switching: cost. Customer stated: “the primary thing was cost… with the increase it was a bit of a significant one.”
Customer indicated that without the pricing increase, they likely would have renewed.

Subject: Enterprise Renewal Call

What.Name: Bendigo and Adelaide Bank - Khoros - Renewal - 1707443 - 4_2026

Description:
Meeting Summary
* Meeting date and time: 03/25/2026
* Meeting attendees: Keith Partridge (Customer – Bendigo Bank), Sebastian Desand (Renewals, Trilogy/IgniteTech)
* Meeting length: ~17 minutes
Talking points
Context and issue raised:
* Keith recently joined and is picking up renewal discussions late in the process.
* Internal confusion due to two previous Statements of Work (SOWs), one of which was discontinued 6–9 months ago.
* Concern that renewal pricing includes components no longer in use (specifically “web chat” / “modern chat”).
Product clarification:
* Vendor clarified that:
    * The discontinued “modern chat/web chat” component was non-recurring and is no longer part of the current contract.
    * Current contract (2025) only includes active “social” (care + marketing) components.
* Customer misunderstanding resolved: they are already only paying for the active social elements.
Pricing and budget concerns:
* Customer has no budget for price increase.
* Initial assumption: removing unused components could reduce cost.
* Reality:
    * Auto-renewal maintains same entitlements but increases price.
    * No removable unused components remain to reduce cost.
* Customer realization:
    * “That might address first problem… but it sounds like that's not even an option.”
Renewal options explained:
* Option 1: Auto-renewal
    * Same legacy terms and entitlements
    * Higher price
* Option 2: New quote (preferred by vendor)
    * New commercial model
    * Same + additional entitlements
    * Includes:
        * Unlimited seats and usage
        * Khoros One (new unified platform)
        * Khoros Community (high-value addition)
* Shift from “like-for-like” pricing to bundled value model.
Customer reaction to new model:
* Customer was unaware of new packaging and added value.
* Internal communication gap on customer side acknowledged.
* Key realization:
    * “It’s actually a different model. It’s not like-for-like.”
* Noted that this may change internal discussions.
Commercial considerations:
* Current estimated cost: ~205K vs ~165K previously.
* Concern about ongoing annual increases with 1-year term.
* 3-year option offers price lock and additional benefits (e.g., AI usage).
Timeline and urgency:
* Renewal date imminent (April 3).
* Auto-renewal already triggered; cannot be reversed after renewal date.
* Customer has short window to evaluate alternative quote.
Next steps agreed:
* Sebastian to send renewal presentation outlining differences in models.
* Keith to review internally and regroup with team.
* Decision to be made urgently before renewal deadline.

Insights
* Renewal likelihood: Likely to win
    * Customer is not actively looking to churn but is constrained by budget and confusion.
    * New value proposition may positively influence decision if understood internally.
* Key risks:
    * Budget constraint (no allocation for price increase)
    * Late-stage engagement and lack of internal alignment
    * Poor communication of new pricing model/value
* Customer statement:
    * “There is not budget for an increase.”
* Key opportunity:
    * Reframing from price increase → value expansion (bundled offering, unlimited usage, added products)
    * Education gap identified; potential to recover deal through clearer positioning
* Notable dynamic:
    * Customer initially believed they were overpaying for unused products (incorrect assumption)
    * Once clarified, conversation shifted to value vs cost
* Requests likely difficult to accommodate:
    * Reducing price by removing components (already removed)
    * Avoiding price increase entirely

Actions from the Meeting
* Sebastian Desand:
    * Send renewal presentation outlining new vs legacy model
    * Support clarification of entitlements and pricing
    * Remain available for follow-up discussion
* Keith Partridge:
    * Review renewal options internally
    * Align with stakeholders on budget and model differences
    * Reassess decision in light of new information

Subject: Renewal Call
Description:
read.ai | Sheel Patel and James Stothard (Meet Meeting) | Tue. Mar 24, 2026

Full Notes and Transcript: https://app.read.ai/analytics/meetings/01KMGFP96SMDMY9G1T80ZX6KZE

Meeting ID: 01KMGFP96SMDMY9G1T80ZX6KZE

3 Participants | James Stothard, Marina Franco, Sheel Patel

----

Summary:

The meeting reviewed Trilogy's renewal terms and pricing for Khoros following the acquisition. James Stothard explained Trilogy's renewed role in handling renewals and centralized services and described changes to paperwork and standard terms since Khoros changed ownership. Sheel Patel and Marina Franco confirmed their procurement roles, said they originate renewal conversations, and will engage marketing and product users for technical input.

Discussion covered available product tiers (standard and platinum success, plus a new enterprise plus tier) and the current usage baseline (reported as 500,000 annual visits and 10 million API calls per month). James emphasized the need to verify actual usage to size pricing appropriately and disclosed a 25% price increase tied to post-acquisition business and product investment. He also outlined that multi-year contract options can include unlimited visits/API calls as a discount incentive. Outstanding items include final sizing based on verified usage and the customer's decision on the appropriate product tier, which will determine final pricing.

----

Chapters & Topics:

Small talk and connection checks
Meeting purpose and roles
-Trilogy will handle the renewal process and has standardized terms attached to the quote rather than separate MSAs
-The client’s renewal is occurring under new ownership; paperwork and some terms have changed since the acquisition
Client role descriptions and renewal context
-The client prefers one-year terms as a company policy and will decide internally whether to renew or not before the end of April notice window
Contract mechanics and product options
-James will provide a written quote and product summary that includes options (standard, platinum success, enterprise plus) for the product team to review
Usage metrics and pricing details
-Current licensed usage on the contract is 500,000 annual visitors and 10 million API calls per month, which needs verification by the client
-Multi-year terms (3 or 5 years) offer pricing benefits including potential unlimited visits/API calls that may be attractive if usage is expected to grow
-Trilogy has implemented a 25% price increase to support product investments following the acquisition
Term lengths and renewal timing
Next steps and meeting close

----

Action Items:

-James Stothard will offer and coordinate demos or a separate call with the client’s product team and Trilogy’s product team if requested
-Sheel Patel will verify current platform usage metrics (annual visitors and monthly API calls) with the product/marketing team
-James Stothard will include pricing details and the one-page recap of product options and benefits in the follow-up email
-Sheel Patel and Marina Franco will review the quote and product summary internally and provide a decision or feedback in approximately two weeks
-James Stothard will send a written quote with attached standard terms and a product summary to the client

----

Key Questions:

-Is this the first renewal since Khoros was acquired?
-What are the current licensed usage limits on the contract?
-Will the client verify how much the product is being used to determine pricing needs?
-What is the notice period or timing required to proceed with renewal?
-Does the client prefer one-year or multi-year contract terms?

Subject: Check-in Call
Description:
read.ai | Quest Diagnostics - Acorn Renewal | 2026-03-24T14:59:55+00:00

Full Notes and Transcript: https://app.read.ai/analytics/meetings/01KMG5AZ7R7PKXEN29EBP0D5P5

Meeting ID: 01KMG5AZ7R7PKXEN29EBP0D5P5

3 Participants | James Quigley, jonathan.c.boydston@questdiagnostics.com, Paul

----

Summary:

The meeting reviewed Quest's use of Acorn and renewal options for the upcoming contract period. James Quigley and Paul discussed that Quest primarily uses Acorn to calculate testing costs, and Paul was tasked with obtaining one-year and three-year renewal quotes to inform the decision. They reviewed support tiers: standard support and a platinum tier that includes a managed upgrade covering the technical migration. Paul reported the managed upgrade work has not been completed and the current installation remains on a 2013 version, with technical difficulties hindering progress.

The conversation covered contract timing and procurement logistics, noting the current contract expires in July and decisions will be made in the months leading up to that date. James committed to sending pricing for both standard and platinum support along with explanatory materials, to verify whether the vendor legal/entity information needs updating, and to provide updated procurement paperwork, including a W-9 if required. No final procurement decision was recorded during the discussion.

----

Chapters & Topics:

Introductions and Call Setup
Purpose of Call and Product Use
-Quest uses ACORN mainly to calculate clinical testing costs
Upgrade Status and Support Tiers
-The current ACORN installation is on an outdated 2013 version and upgrade work is not yet complete
-There are two support tiers: standard and platinum, with platinum including a managed upgrade service
-James will send pricing comparisons and details for standard vs. platinum support for Paul to review
Pricing, Onboarding Timing, and Next Steps
-The contract renewal decision will be made prior to the contract expiry in July
Procurement and Vendor Entity Changes
-The vendor/service provider name has changed and may require procurement updates (previous billing to ESW operations)
Information Exchange and Documentation Commitments
-James will supply updated vendor paperwork (e.g., W‑9) if needed to update Quest’s procurement records
Closing

----

Action Items:

-James Quigley will send standard and platinum pricing and an outline of what is included with platinum support
-Paul will provide procurement contact information or the appropriate payee details when it is time to update vendor records
-James Quigley will verify whether the vendor/payee change requires updated procurement paperwork and will send an updated W‑9 if needed
-James Quigley will email the pricing and informational materials this afternoon for Paul to review

----

Key Questions:

-Was the upgrade to a newer ACORN version completed?
-Is the difference between standard and platinum primarily the managed upgrade?
-What is the decision timeline for the renewal?

Subject: Cancellation Call
Description:
Meeting Summary
* Meeting date and time: 03/23/2026
* Meeting attendees: Adam (Harley-Davidson), Sebastian Desand (Renewals, Trilogy/IgniteTech)
* Meeting length: ~19 minutes

Talking points
Purpose of the call:
* Customer requested short-term extension options:
* 1-month extension
* Month-to-month flexibility (up to ~4 months)
* Secondary objective: gather feedback on decision not to renew Khoros.
Renewal status:
* Customer has decided not to renew Khoros.
* Currently in final stages of vendor selection (down to two finalists).
* Decision expected within 1–2 weeks.
Customer feedback on non-renewal:
* RFP initiated to explore market after long-term use (since Spredfast days).
* Key issues:
* Poor transition post-IgniteTech acquisition
* Lack of clarity on ownership, contacts, and product roadmap
* Account instability and rotating contacts
* Open support tickets and unresolved issues
* Product/vision concerns:
* Poor RFP/demo experience (overly technical, engineering-led)
* Lack of clear value articulation
* Customer unable to justify investment internally due to unclear ROI
* Customer statement:
* “We really saw a rocky handoff after… the IgniteTech acquisition.”
* “It wasn’t clear who we were supposed to be talking to, and what was going on, and what was the roadmap.”
* “I couldn’t justify… investing in a tool that we can’t actually say how it’s going to make us better.”
Decision drivers:
* Combination of factors (no single driver):
* Account management instability
* Lack of roadmap clarity
* Poor communication during RFP
* Customer indicated outcome could have been different with better engagement:
* “Had somebody… had a higher-level conversation, that may have gone differently.”
Extension request outcome:
* Vendor confirmed strict policy:
* Minimum contract term is 12 months
* No 1-month or month-to-month extensions allowed
* Escalation possible but highly unlikely to succeed (no precedent).
* Vendor advised focusing on accelerating transition instead.
Transition status:
* Contract end date: May 5 (renewal May 6).
* ~6 weeks remaining.
* Customer preparing formal non-renewal notice per contract.
Next steps discussed:
* Sebastian to (formally) escalate extension request, with expectation of rejection.
* Response expected midweek.
* Customer to continue vendor selection and transition planning.
* Suggested: engage account team for any alternative support options (e.g., services workaround).

Insights
* Renewal likelihood: None (confirmed churn)
* Key churn drivers:
* Poor post-acquisition transition experience
* Lack of clear roadmap and strategic communication
* Weak RFP/demo execution (too technical, not value-driven)
* Account instability and unclear ownership
* Secondary factors:
* Long tenure led to market re-evaluation (RFP)
* Competitive landscape likely stronger
* Critical takeaway:
* Loss was not due to product capability alone, but failure to clearly communicate value and direction
* Sales/solutioning approach during RFP significantly impacted outcome
* Customer sentiment:
* Constructive and professional
* Open to reconsidering in future RFP cycles
* “Rooting” for product improvement
* Requests not aligned with policy:
* Short-term extension (1 month or month-to-month)

Actions from the Meeting
* Sebastian Desand:
* Submit escalation request for short-term extension (despite low likelihood)
* Respond to customer by midweek (Wednesday)
* Suggest engagement with account team for alternative support
* Adam (Harley-Davidson):
* Continue vendor selection (finalists, decision in 1–2 weeks)
* Submit formal non-renewal notice per contract
* Explore transition support options with account team
* Internal (supplier):
* Account team to explore any non-standard support options during transition
* Capture feedback on RFP/demo approach and post-acquisition transition

Subject: Cancellation Call
Description:
Meeting Summary
* Meeting date and time: 03/23/2026
* Meeting attendees: Marlaine Maldonado (Customer), Sebastian Desand (Renewals, Trilogy/IgniteTech)
* Meeting length: ~14 minutes

Talking points
Cancellation status:
* Leadership approved honoring the customer’s cancellation request despite initial denial.
* Customer confirmed receipt of updated email stating cancellation is being processed.
Reasons for non-renewal:
* Pricing (primary driver):
* Renewal quote higher than expected and not feasible within current budget.
* Customer statement: renewal pricing “was also higher than we anticipated, and it's not something that we can fit into this year's budget.”
* Product limitations / issues:
* API duplication issues impacting Talkwalker integration
* Missing analytics and reporting capabilities
* Internal factors:
* Budget constraints
* Team restructuring
* Overall sentiment:
* “It just felt like we weren’t getting what we needed, especially with the cost.”
Pricing expectations:
* Customer would have considered renewal if pricing remained closer to previous levels.
* Perception that current pricing is not competitive vs. other platforms.
* Ongoing frustration with incremental cost increases over time.
* Customer highlighted difficulty justifying increasing costs internally to executives.
Commercial model feedback:
* Preference for simpler, more consolidated platform offering at a reasonable cost.
* Frustration with past negotiations involving trade-offs between features and budget.
Alternative solutions:
* Customer currently evaluating other vendors; no final decision yet.
* Will continue using Khoros until contract end.
Contract and timeline clarification:
* Confusion over contract end date clarified during call:
* Actual end date: May 7 (based on May 8 renewal date)
* Misinterpretation due to date format in cancellation email
Transition and offboarding:
* Customer will begin transition planning.
* Data access:
* Most data expected to be accessible within platform
* Additional support available via account manager if needed
* Account manager (Amir) identified as main contact for transition support

Insights
* Renewal likelihood: None (confirmed churn)
* Key churn drivers:
* Pricing increase (primary driver)
* Product gaps (API duplication, analytics limitations)
* Perceived lack of value relative to cost
* Internal budget constraints
* Supporting factors:
* Frustration with pricing model over time
* Need for more consolidated/efficient solution
* Notable dynamic:
* Unlike other churn cases, pricing is the leading factor, with product issues reinforcing the decision
* Positive/neutral signals:
* Customer interaction remained professional and constructive
* No major complaints about support or relationship (contrast to other accounts)
* Requests likely not alignable with policy:
* Expectation to maintain or reduce pricing to prior levels despite broader pricing increases
Actions from the Meeting
* Sebastian Desand:
* Confirm and document cancellation acceptance
* Clarify contract end date (completed)
* Ensure internal alignment for transition support via account manager
* Marlaine Maldonado:
* Proceed with transition planning
* Evaluate alternative vendors
* Reach out to account manager (Amir) for any data extraction or support needs
* Internal (supplier):
* Amir (Account Manager) to support offboarding and data access
* Ensure smooth transition and fulfillment of contractual obligations

Subject: Check-in Call
Description:
read.ai | Stepchange DNN Renewal (Meet Meeting) | Mon. Mar 23, 2026

Full Notes and Transcript: https://app.read.ai/analytics/meetings/01KMD57FVSBX0G98DMX99Q4Y64

Meeting ID: 01KMD57FVSBX0G98DMX99Q4Y64

3 Participants | James Stothard, Jenny Simpson, Shanny

----

Summary:

The meeting reviewed the upcoming DNN contract renewal and the charity’s intention to migrate off DNN as part of a wider transformation programme. Attendees introduced roles: Shanny as the new commercial and supplier manager for DCS who will own supplier management and renewal reviews, and Jenny as the website manager confirming the website team currently uses DNN but that the exit timescale is uncertain due to the broader transformation.

James explained Trilogy/IgniteTech’s group structure and that Trilogy handles renewals centrally, described standard term options of one, three, or five years, and noted the current practice of a significant annual price uplift reported at about 25% on renewal. The group confirmed the contractual timeline, with the renewal date in August and a 60‑day cancellation notice required in June, and discussed commercial and public‑sector pricing considerations and contractual options in light of the charity’s long‑term migration plans.

----

Chapters & Topics:

Attendance and connection issues
Introductions and roles
-Shanny is the new commercial and supplier manager who will review supplier contracts and lead renewal guidance for DCS.
Strategy to move off DNN and constraints
-The meeting focused on the DNN renewal and exploring options given the charity’s intention to move away from DNN.
-Trilogy (central services) manages renewals while IgniteTech runs the DNN product day-to-day.
-The charity’s longer-term plan is to exit DNN but the transformation program creates timing uncertainty for migration decisions.
Renewal dates and notice period
-The renewal date is in August and the contract requires a 60-day (approx. June) cancellation notice.
Commercial terms and pricing policy
-Action agreed: James will send the current commercial agreement and a three-year quote for review.
Next steps and close

----

Action Items:

-Shanny will review the commercial agreement and return recommendations on the commercial position and public-sector pricing options.
-James Stothard will send a three-year quote and the current commercial agreement to the participants.
-Shanny will schedule a follow-up call with James and Janine to discuss options after her review.

----

Key Questions:

-Has the process to move away from DNN already started?
-Is the current agreement a private commercial deal or procured through Crown Commercial?
-When is the renewal due and what is the cancellation deadline?
